source: Bluetooth Core Specification, Vol 3, Part H (Security Manager Protocol)

0x01.name: Passkey Entry Failed
0x01.description: The user did not correctly enter the requested passkey during pairing.
common_causes: User mistyped the passkey
User cancelled the passkey entry dialog

0x02.name: OOB Not Available
0x02.description: Out-of-band pairing data was required but not available.
common_causes: Pairing method negotiated to OOB but one side has no OOB data (e.g. NFC tag not read)

0x03.name: Authentication Requirements
0x03.description: The pairing method couldn't satisfy the authentication requirements (e.g. MITM protection) requested by one of the devices.
common_causes: One device requires MITM protection (authenticated pairing) but the IO capabilities of the pair only support Just Works
Bonding flags mismatch between initiator and responder

0x04.name: Confirm Value Failed
0x04.description: The confirm value computed during pairing did not match, indicating tampering or a protocol error.
common_causes: Data corruption on the link during pairing
Potential MITM interference
Stack bug in confirm/random value generation

0x05.name: Pairing Not Supported
0x05.description: The device does not support pairing at all.
common_causes: Peer device has security disabled or doesn't implement SMP

0x06.name: Encryption Key Size
0x06.description: The negotiated encryption key size was below the minimum required by one of the devices.
common_causes: Peer requested a short key size below the local minimum security policy (commonly an attack-downgrade attempt, or just a legacy/low-power peer)

0x07.name: Command Not Supported
0x07.description: The SMP command received is not supported by this device.
common_causes: SMP version mismatch between stacks

0x08.name: Unspecified Reason
0x08.description: Pairing failed for a reason that doesn't have a more specific code -- a catch-all failure.
common_causes: Internal stack error during pairing not otherwise classified

0x09.name: Repeated Attempts
0x09.description: Pairing was attempted too many times in a short window and was rejected as a precaution against brute-force attacks.
common_causes: Automatic retry loop hammering pairing after each failure
Possible brute-force passkey guessing attempt

0x0A.name: Invalid Parameters
0x0A.description: One of the pairing parameters was invalid or unacceptable.
common_causes: Malformed Pairing Request/Response PDU from a buggy stack

0x0B.name: DHKey Check Failed
0x0B.description: The DHKey check value in LE Secure Connections pairing did not match.
common_causes: Public key exchange corrupted or tampered with
Possible MITM attack during Secure Connections pairing

0x0C.name: Numeric Comparison Failed
0x0C.description: The user rejected the numeric comparison (the displayed codes did not match, or the user pressed 'No').
common_causes: User intentionally rejected pairing
Display showing different values on each side, indicating tampering or a UI bug

0x0D.name: BR/EDR Pairing in Progress
0x0D.description: Pairing was rejected because a BR/EDR pairing procedure is already in progress on this link.
common_causes: Cross-transport pairing attempted while classic pairing hasn't finished

0x0E.name: Cross-transport Key Derivation/Generation not allowed
0x0E.description: Cross-transport key derivation (deriving an LE key from a BR/EDR key or vice versa) was not permitted in this context.
common_causes: Security policy disallows cross-transport key derivation for this pairing

0x0F.name: Key Rejected
0x0F.description: The distributed key was rejected by the receiving device.
common_causes: Key didn't meet local security policy (e.g. too weak) and was refused
